generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLidar : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor Hokuyo UST10LX short‑range 2D LiDAR mounted above the front bumper, 10 Hz update rate, 270° field of view"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoUST10LXPointCloudTrans
]

instance IntelDeepSenseD435i : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel DeepSense D435i depth camera centered in rear bumper, 720 Hz depth rate, 640×480 resolution, 91° horizontal FOV"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelD435iDepthImageTrans
]

instance IMU : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IMU providing acceleration with ±1.6 g accuracy and gyroscope at ±12 000 °/s"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IMUDataTrans
]

instance VehicleECU : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle ECU sensor delivering motor torque, steering angle, and servo position over CAN‑FD, with CAN ID allocation per ISO 23247"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTrans
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑Virtual data transmitted
instance HokuyoUST10LXPointCloudTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLidar
]

instance IntelD435iDepthImageTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepSenseD435i
]

instance IMUDataTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IMU
]

instance VehicleStateDataTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleECU
]

instance ObjectTracksTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel
]

instance ProjectedPathTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel
]

instance ACCCommandTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AdaptiveCruiseControlModel
]

instance MotorTorqueCommandTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderESC
]

instance MotorPositionCommandTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Nod18StepMotor
]

instance SteeringAngleCommandTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SixMMLinearSteeringServo
]

// C10: Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data from Hokuyo UST10LX sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoUST10LXPointCloudTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth image data from Intel D435i depth camera"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IntelD435iDepthImageTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance IMUData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Accelerometer and gyroscope data from the vehicle IMU"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IMUDataTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor torque, steering angle, and servo position telemetry from Vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filter on LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCEnabler
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path computed from LiDAR clustering results"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedPathTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance ACCCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control command comprising desired speed and acceleration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ACCCommandTrans
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCCommandReceiverEnabler
]

// C10: Models
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model performing DBSCAN clustering on raw LiDAR point clouds and tracking objects with a Kalman filter"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC model determining desired speed from lead vehicle distance and vehicle state to compute command"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCEnabler
]

// C3: Acting components
instance VedderESC : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller regulating motor torque via PWM, 0‑100 % throttle, 10 kW current limit, 80 °C shutdown"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTorqueCommandTrans
]

instance Nod18StepMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "NEMA 17 stepper motor shaft coupled to the drive unit for propulsion"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionCommandTrans
]

instance SixMMLinearSteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "60 mm linear steering servo translating steering angle commands via CAN‑FD with ±45° steering angle range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleCommandTrans
]

// C17: Insights
instance LiveVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live insight of vehicle state including throttle, brake, and steering commands"
]

instance LiveProjectedPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live projected driving path visualized from LiDAR clustering and tracking"
]

// C17: Actions
instance SaveTelemetryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to save current telemetry data to persistent storage"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to generate domain‑specific telemetry reports"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetACCEnabledAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control remotely"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set a desired cruise speed through remote interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to issue an emergency braking command"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ACCCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to send ACC command to vehicle ECU via remote supervision"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that routes raw LiDAR data to the clustering and tracking model"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel
]

instance CloudACCEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud‑based offloading enabler for Adaptive Cruise Control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ACCCommandReceiverEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that receives ACC command data for remote supervision and control"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that streams telemetry and perception data to the data monitoring service"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that forwards remote management actions to vehicle control interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Web‑based user interface displaying real‑time vehicle state, projected path, and telemetry, with capabilities to save data and generate reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveVehicleStateInsight, LiveProjectedPathInsight, SaveTelemetryAction, GenerateReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that offloads ACC computation to cloud resources, computing desired speed from lead vehicle distance and providing command back to the vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:provides SetDesiredSpeedAction, ACCCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and issue emergency braking via low‑latency communication"
    DTDFVocab:provides SetACCEnabledAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, IssueEmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Devices are required to share a common clock reference to enable computation offloading. The system upper‑bound clock skew must not exceed 100 ms due to hardware constraints, though safety‑critical vehicular standards often demand tighter synchronization. Synchronization granularity is therefore set to 100 ms time‑stamps for data exchange between physical and digital entities."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The design follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping each Front‑End Entity (FE) to an individual ROS2 package that encapsulates one or more ROS2 nodes. The constellation comprises a physical resource layer (Physical Twin Entity hosting compute, sensors and actuators, including point‑cloud clustering & tracking), a control/data layer (Digital Twin Entity supplying data management and actuation logic), and a human‑interaction layer (User Entity providing 3D visualization and control‑panel interfaces). Resource management is achieved by binding each FE to the appropriate hardware resources (e.g., sensor drivers on the Physical Twin node, database services on the Digital Twin node). System‑wide coordination is handled via ROS2 pub/sub for data streams, ROS2 services and action interfaces for command execution, and a shared timestamping mechanism to maintain the 100 ms synchronization precision."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is mandated to be real‑time and high‑fidelity as per ISO 23247 Part 1. Validation involves cross‑checking model outputs against live sensor streams via a unit‑test harness that asserts within ±5 % of measured values. Verification follows a tolerance budget: network latency is constrained to ≤100 ms, as defined by requirement R7, and system clocks are synchronized with a Precision Time Protocol (PTP) network with 100 µs accuracy per requirement R8. Uncertainty sources such as sensor noise and model parameter drift are quantified using Kalman filter covariance propagation for the point‑cloud tracker and analytical uncertainty bounds for the ACC module. Error detection is performed by runtime consistency checks that compare redundant sensor feeds; incongruities trigger an immediate fault flag, with correction through sensor fusion error‑model lookup tables. Quality assurance processes involve static code analysis of the controller logic, continuous integration pipelines that deploy the DT to a test harness, and pre‑deployment simulation runs in a physics engine to validate safety invariants such as rollover and collision avoidance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Software components inside the Digital Twin platform are connected through a ROS 2 mesh that manages inter‑component communication. For communication between the vehicle (Physical Twin) and the Digital Twin node, a Wi‑Fi link is employed; the underlying transport layer is unspecified beyond the use of a UDP‑like datagram service as mentioned in the architecture diagram, although the exact protocol (e.g. UDP or a higher‑level protocol) is not fully detailed in the source. The Data Translation component ensures that all messages are serialized and properly typed before transmission, maintaining semantic consistency across the heterogeneous data streams. No concrete bandwidth, latency, reliability, or redundancy parameters are specified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is instantiated on a separate computational platform from the vehicle’s on‑board electronics, effectively decoupling the Physical Twin Entity (vehicle hardware) from the Digital Twin Entity (off‑loaded compute). The server or edge device that hosts the Digital Twin runs the ROS 2 middleware and all associated data‑processing components, utilizing cloud or edge compute resources as needed. Each entity is deployed on distinct hardware to isolate processing loads and to facilitate scaling, although specific storage, memory, or scalability dimensions are not detailed in the provided text. The architecture supports a modular deployment model where the Digital Twin can be extended or relocated without impacting the vehicle’s on‑board hardware."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Complies with ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing, part 2 defining an entity‑based reference architecture. The architecture comprises Data Collection and Device Control, Digital Twin, and User Entities, each composed of Functional Entities responsible for distinct tasks (e.g., data collection, actuator control, data transformation, user interfacing). The standard allows implementation with OPC Unified Architecture and adheres to ISO 23247’s information‑exchange specifications for interoperable digital twin solutions."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for an adaptive cruise control (ACC) vehicle is defined as safety‑critical and must comply with the safety lifecycle and hazard analysis methodology outlined in ISO 26262‑1:2018.  The control strategy and performance verification are governed by ISO 15622, which specifies that the ACC system must reproduce vehicle state data on a device external to the vehicle with pre‑defined accuracy limits.  For V2X communications the Digital Twin must meet the end‑to‑end latency bound defined in 3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15, typically requiring an upper limit of 100 ms (plus an acceptable jitter tolerance).  Consequently, the Digital Twin must implement a deterministic communication stack that guarantees these latency constraints for safety‑related messages.  The security of the V2X interface is not explicitly defined in the extracted documents, so the Digital Twin follows the minimal security considerations recommended by 3GPP, namely integrity and authenticity of V2X messages (e.g., using message authentication codes) and protection against replay attacks.  In summary, the safety and security posture of the Digital Twin is driven by ISO 26262‑1 safety integrity level specifications, ISO 15622 control‑strategy test procedures, and the V2X latency bounds from 3GPP TS 22.185. No additional cybersecurity protocols are prescribed in the provided materials."
]
